name: sop-generator
description: "Use when: writing a complete Statement of Purpose for a specific school. End-to-end SOP creation with narrative consistency, school-specific tailoring, and quality scoring."
---

# 🎓 SOP Generator — Complete Statement of Purpose Workflow

Transform your background into a compelling, school-specific Statement of Purpose in 7 steps.

---

## 📋 Workflow Overview

```
Step 1: Extract Core Story
   ↓ [Review narrative threads]
Step 2: Draft Inflection Point
   ↓ [Write pivotal moment paragraph]
Step 3: Generate Full SOP
   ↓ [650-word draft for school]
Step 4: Get Admissions Feedback
   ↓ [Adcom review simulation with score]
Step 5: Strengthen Weak Paragraphs
   ↓ [Iterate on specific sections]
Step 6: Differentiation Check
   ↓ [Compare with other schools' SOPs]
Step 7: Polish & Word Limit
   ↓ [Final edit to exact length]
READY FOR SUBMISSION
```

---

## ✨ Features

- **Narrative Consistency**: Ensures same core story flows across all school versions
- **School-Specific Customization**: Tailors talking points, course examples, career goals per school
- **Quality Scoring**: Simulates admissions committee feedback with /10 score
- **Progressive Refinement**: Build → Review → Iterate → Polish workflow
- **Comparison Engine**: Detects duplicate/generic passages across multiple SOPs

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### Minimal (2 hours)
```
1. /extract-core-story [paste resume]
2. /draft-full-sop [school name + core narrative]
3. /adcom-review-simulation [paste draft]
4. /cut-to-word-limit [trim to exact length]
→ Ready to submit
```

### Comprehensive (4 hours)
```
1. /extract-core-story
2. /draft-inflection-point [write pivotal moment]
3. /draft-full-sop [NYU version]
4. /adcom-review-simulation [get score]
5. /strengthen-weak-paragraphs [fix weak areas]
6. /draft-full-sop [Rochester version]
7. /differentiation-check [NYU vs Rochester]
8. /cut-to-word-limit [both versions]
→ Ready for 2 schools
```

---

- `templates/school-specific-checklist.md` — What to customize per school

- `examples/before-after-adcom-feedback.md` — How edits improve score

- `reference/school-talking-points.md` — Unique selling points per school
- `reference/common-mistakes.md` — What to avoid in SOPs
- `reference/word-choice-guide.md` — Strong verbs, active voice examples

---

## 🎯 Inputs Required

Before starting, have ready:
- ✅ Your resume (full text)
- ✅ Target school name + program
- ✅ 2–3 reasons why this specific school
- ✅ 1 specific course you want to take (from program website)
- ✅ Your 2-year and 5-year career goals

---

## 📊 Output Checklist

After this workflow, you'll have:
- ✅ Core narrative (reusable across all schools)
- ✅ School-specific SOP draft (650 words)
- ✅ Admissions committee score + feedback
- ✅ List of weak paragraphs to strengthen
- ✅ Comparison notes (what's unique vs generic)
- ✅ Final polished version (exact word count)

---

## 💡 Pro Tips

✅ **Do:**
- Start with `/extract-core-story` — identifies your unique threads
- Use `/adcom-review-simulation` before `/differentiation-check` — improves quality first
- Create all 4 school SOPs before final polish — easier to compare while fresh
- Save your core narrative separately — reuse across all schools

❌ **Don't:**
- Copy paste SOP across schools — `/differentiation-check` will catch it
- Skip `/adcom-review-simulation` — this is your quality gate
- Finalize word count before content is strong — quality > length

---

## 🎓 School-Specific Guides

### NYU SPS
- Emphasis: Data science + business analytics depth
- Unique talking point: "Management + Analytics" integration
- Tone: Ambitious, technically rigorous

### Simon Business School (Rochester)
- Emphasis: Marketing analytics, customer intelligence
- Unique talking point: Marketing-specific analytics program
- Tone: Balanced (analytics + strategy)

### Rutgers Business School
- Emphasis: Consumer insights, digital analytics, real-world applications
- Unique talking point: Industry partnerships & internship opportunities
- Tone: Practical, outcome-focused

### CUNY Baruch (Zicklin)
- Emphasis: Marketing fundamentals + analytics integration
- Unique talking point: NYC location, diverse student body, strong recruiting
- Tone: Community-oriented, growth-focused

---

## 🔧 Troubleshooting

**Q: My SOP sounds generic even after `/adcom-review-simulation`**
A: Use `/strengthen-weak-paragraphs` on specific sections. Too much passive voice? Wrong school emphasis? Check `reference/common-mistakes.md`

**Q: I'm repeating the same stories across schools**
A: Run `/differentiation-check` — it will flag duplicates. Then rephrase using different angles from your resume.

**Q: I'm stuck on word count**
A: Use `/cut-to-word-limit` — it ruthlessly edits while preserving key arguments. Then `/adcom-review-simulation` again to ensure quality didn't drop.

**Q: How do I know if my SOP is competitive?**
A: Target score: 7.5+/10 from `/adcom-review-simulation`. If below 7, rework using feedback. Check `reference/sop-scoring-rubric.md` to understand the gaps.
